title: T-054 — E2E exception gates: blocked release / allergen / over-consumption / completion (§1.1A, SCR-08-03, MODAL-08-05)
prompt:
# T-054 — E2E exception gates

PRD: docs/prd/08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §1.1A, §8.1 SCR-08-03, MODAL-08-04 (Resume), MODAL-08-05 (Complete), §6 D3 (closed_production_strict), §7.2 FR-08-E2-006 (over-consumption approval), §6 D10 (allergen gate)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Playwright spec covering the four negative paths Production must enforce: (1) missing/blocked canonical release row blocks START with typed blockers, (2) allergen changeover required redirects to SCR-08-03, (3) over-consumption requires Shift Lead approval, (4) `closed_production_strict_v1` failure on Complete requires Prod Manager override with reason.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/e2e/production.spec.ts adds `test.describe('exception gates')` with four `test()` cases (one per gate).
2. apps/web/e2e/fixtures/production.ts adds gate-specific seeders: `seedBlockedRelease`, `seedAllergenRequired`, `seedOverConsumptionAttempt`, `seedIncompleteOutputs`.
3. Per gate, the spec asserts: (a) UI state (button disabled / 423 redirect / approval modal / override modal), (b) typed `release_blockers` or `gate_failures` payload, (c) DB invariants — no `wo_executions.started_at` / `completed_at` written for blocked transitions; consumed/output rows match assertions.
4. Override paths (Shift Lead approve, Prod Manager force complete) are also exercised with PIN-confirmed flows and produce audit_log rows.
5. Playwright config retains trace/screenshot/video; tenant-scoped cleanup after run.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a WO whose canonical release is `pending_technical_approval`, when the user navigates to the WO and tries Start, then the Start button is disabled, typed `release_blockers` are visible, the Open-in-Planning deep-link is present, and no `wo_executions.started_at` is written.
2. Given an allergen changeover requirement, when Start is invoked, then the spec is redirected to `/production/changeover/<event_id>`, and after walking cleaning + ATP + dual sign-off the second Start succeeds without selecting a new BOM/spec.
3. Given an over-consumption attempt exceeding 5% tolerance, when the operator submits the consume-to-WO call, then it returns 409 `requires_approval=true`, the Shift Lead approval modal opens, a PIN-confirmed approve produces an `audit_log` row, and the consumption is recorded; alternatively, a Prod Manager `closed_production_strict_v1` override on Complete is captured with `override_reason_code` in audit_log.

## Test strategy
- RED: write the four `exception gates` test cases + seeders before the corresponding API / UI slices exist; each case must fail with the expected blocker reason.
- Run `pnpm --filter web exec playwright test production.spec.ts -g "exception gates" --trace=on`
- Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not bypass typed blocker contracts (each gate must return a typed payload, not a generic 500).
- Do not let an unauthorized role override `closed_production_strict_v1` (Prod Manager only).
- Do not skip audit_log on approve/override paths.
- Do not allow a newer BOM/spec to be selected after the allergen gate clears.

pipeline_inputs.description: Playwright spec covering 4 negative gates: blocked release, allergen changeover, over-consumption approval, closed_production_strict override; typed blockers and audit_log emission asserted.
pipeline_inputs.details: Four `test()` cases inside one describe block; per-gate seeder fixtures keep the spec deterministic. Gate behaviour asserts both UI state and DB invariants. Override flows (Shift Lead approve / Prod Manager force-complete) require PIN and produce audit_log rows. No newer BOM/spec selection after allergen gate clears (canonical snapshot preserved per T-001).
